CheckTitle: AI Agent Must Use Distributed Tracing with W3C Trace Context
CheckType: Software and Configuration Checks/AI Agent Security Best Practices
Description: **AI Agent Observability**. Verifies that AI agents propagate W3C trace context (traceparent/tracestate headers) across all service calls. Without distributed tracing, it is impossible to reconstruct end-to-end request timelines across multi-agent systems, making incident investigation extremely difficult.
Risk: Multi-agent systems involve chains of service calls across multiple components. Without distributed tracing, security teams cannot reconstruct the sequence of events during an incident, identify which agent initiated a malicious chain, or determine the full scope of data access across a complex agent workflow.

Code.Other:
1. Integrate an OpenTelemetry SDK into the agent and configure trace export to a tracing backend (Jaeger, Zipkin, AWS X-Ray, Datadog APM).
2. Propagate W3C traceparent and tracestate headers on all outbound HTTP/gRPC calls.
3. Include agent ID, tool name, and LLM model in trace span attributes.
4. Configure trace sampling at 100% for security-sensitive operations.

Recommendation.Text: Implement OpenTelemetry-based distributed tracing with W3C trace context propagation across all AI agent service calls. Export traces to a centralised tracing backend for incident investigation and performance analysis.

Notes: ASPM-083 check from the Prowler ASPM check suite.
